title: Never share your OTP
tip: Your OTP (One Time Password) is like the key to your bank locker. No bank, government officer, or helpline will EVER ask for it. If someone asks — it is a scam. Hang up immediately.

title: Two-step verification
tip: Go to WhatsApp → Settings → Account → Two-step verification → Enable. This adds a 6-digit PIN that protects your account even if someone gets your SIM card.

title: Receiving money needs no PIN
tip: You only enter your UPI or Bank PIN when SENDING money. If someone says 'Enter your PIN to receive money', that is fraud — they are actually withdrawing from your account.

title: Use a passphrase
tip: Instead of a hard-to-remember password like 'P@ss123', try a phrase you love: 'MyGardenHas12Roses!' — long, easy to remember, very hard to crack.

title: Check sender email carefully
tip: Scammers copy logos of real banks and companies. Before clicking any link, check the full email address — 'support@bank-secure.com' is fake; only official addresses are real.

title: QR codes can be dangerous
tip: Never scan a QR code sent by a stranger. Fake QR codes can open your payment app and create a request that drains your account — always verify who sent it.

title: Look for the lock icon
tip: Before entering any password or bank details on a website, look for a small padlock (🔒) before the website address. No padlock means the website is not secure.

title: Use caller ID apps safely
tip: Apps like Truecaller help identify unknown numbers, but remember that scammers can fake their names. Always verify independently if someone claims to be from the bank or police.

title: What are cookies?
tip: When websites ask 'Accept cookies?' — these are small files that remember your preferences but also track what you do online. You can always say 'Reject' or 'Only necessary' to share less.

title: Enable SMS alerts for all transactions
tip: Register your mobile number with your bank and enable SMS alerts for every transaction. This way you immediately know if any unauthorized payment happens.

title: Verify before you share
tip: Before forwarding any news or message on WhatsApp, check if it is true on a fact-checking website. Sharing false news can cause unnecessary panic.

title: Payment 'Collect' request fraud
tip: If you get a payment 'collect' request from an unknown person with a reason like 'lottery' or 'prize', REJECT it. They are trying to take money FROM you, not send it to you.

title: Book doctor appointments online
tip: Many hospital apps let you consult real doctors from home — no travel, no waiting. Ask a family member to help you set it up once for easy access to healthcare.

title: Ride-sharing apps are safe
tip: Uber and local taxi apps show your driver's name, photo, and a live trip tracker that you can share with family. Your family can watch your trip in real time for extra safety.

title: Cash on delivery is safer initially
tip: When shopping on a new online store for the first time, choose Cash on Delivery if available. You pay only after seeing the product — reducing the risk of losing money online.
